title: vaccinium coriaceum var. stapfianum
intro: Vaccinium coriaceum var. stapfianum, also known as the high-altitude blueberry, is a type of shrub native to the high-altitude regions of the Himalayas. It is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Vaccinium coriaceum var. stapfianum include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and support immune function. The plant can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, with the fresh berries also being eaten raw or used in traditional medicine.
